name: South Korea
forecastReasoning: Highest robot density globally. Samsung and Hyundai both accelerating humanoid programs. Government 2030 strategy confirms policy tailwind.

name: Singapore
forecastReasoning: Top automation index globally. High wages drive strong ROI. Healthcare pilot signals service sector expansion beyond manufacturing.

name: Germany
forecastReasoning: Strong automotive manufacturing base. IG Metall acknowledgment of 2M jobs at risk confirms labor-cost pressure. High energy costs partially offset by renewable transition.

name: Japan
forecastReasoning: Pioneer industrial robotics ecosystem. Aging workforce creates strong demographic pull. Solar-powered agricultural humanoid signals service sector expansion.

name: Sweden
forecastReasoning: Very high wages accelerate ROI. Cheap hydroelectric power gives robot TCO advantage. Strong tech readiness index.

name: Denmark
forecastReasoning: Highest minimum wage globally creates strongest ROI case. Manufacturing share moderate but growing pharmaceutical automation.

name: Belgium
forecastReasoning: High wages and EU automation framework drive adoption. Chemical and pharma sectors leading.

name: United States
forecastReasoning: Tesla 1000-unit deployment signals industrial tipping point. VC investment in Figure AI confirms investor conviction. Manufacturing share low but logistics sector enormous.

name: China
forecastReasoning: 50,000-unit deployment largest globally. State-backed manufacturing and extremely low energy costs create massive adoption advantage. Manufacturing GDP share second only to KOR.

name: Taiwan
forecastReasoning: Highest manufacturing share globally. Semiconductor and electronics factories already highly automated. TSMC investments signal further adoption.

name: Czech Republic
forecastReasoning: High manufacturing share and EU single market drive adoption. Automotive supply chain integration with German OEMs accelerates timeline.

name: Slovakia
forecastReasoning: Highest manufacturing share in EU. Automotive-heavy economy with Kia, VW, Peugeot plants. Low wages reduce ROI urgency but volume makes aggregate impact large.

name: Hungary
forecastReasoning: Strong automotive base (BMW, Mercedes, Audi). EU regulatory environment and supply chain integration accelerate adoption despite lower wages.

name: Austria
forecastReasoning: High wages and sophisticated manufacturing ecosystem. Strong renewable energy profile reduces robot TCO. KUKA and AVL presence signals automation leadership.

name: Finland
forecastReasoning: Very cheap energy (nuclear/hydro) gives robot TCO edge. High wages and tech readiness. Nokia robotics R&D presence.
